art_style: Found-footage from a technical diver's helmet-mounted camera — a FICTIONAL dramatized reenactment of a real archaeological recovery operation. Vertical 9:16, first-person POV: the frame drifts and sways gently with the diver's slow fin kicks, occasionally a gloved hand, gauge or exhaled bubbles cross the frame. Deep dark water lit only by dive torches and headlamp beams — volumetric light cones full of drifting silt and marine snow, murky green-blue gloom fading to pure black at the edges, mild wide-lens distortion, soft focus shifts, light sensor grain. One or two teammate divers appear ahead as dark silhouettes with glowing torches; their faces are always hidden behind dive masks and regulators, never clearly visible. Atmospheric, ominous, awe-and-dread mood — haunting but never graphic or gory. No on-screen text, no captions, no logos, no watermarks (all overlays are added in post). IMPORTANT: this is a silent visual shot — do NOT render any person speaking, lip-sync or talking to camera; the voice is added separately as narration.
